title: Solar-Variable RO Control Systems
entry_mechanism: Saudi software company developing solar-powered variable-output RO control algorithms with NEOM green desalination as anchor
executive_summary:
Traditional RO desalination operates at steady-state — constant pressure, constant flow, constant power consumption. Solar energy is inherently variable — rising and falling through the day, interrupted by clouds. Marrying solar power with RO desalination requires sophisticated variable-speed pump control, membrane protection algorithms that handle power fluctuations, and intelligent system management that maximises water production during peak solar hours. This is a genuinely novel engineering challenge that Saudi Arabia — with both the world's largest desalination capacity and one of the world's largest solar irradiance — is uniquely positioned to solve.

Node DT-10 targets Saudi-developed solar-variable RO control software and hardware: algorithms that safely operate RO systems across the full power range from 20% to 100% output, protecting membranes during power transients and maximising daily water production from variable solar input. NEOM's green desalination programme — using solar-powered RO exclusively — is the global showcase anchor for this technology.

The global solar-powered desalination market is nascent ($1.2 billion in 2024) but growing at 31.4% CAGR. Saudi Arabia, as the world's leading solar resource country and largest desalination operator, is the natural home for this technology development.

companies.name: ACWA Power
companies.rationale: ACWA Power develops solar-powered desalination globally and is the Saudi anchor investor in this space. ACWA's technical team is the natural development partner.

companies.name: Siemens Energy
companies.rationale: Siemens supplies VFD pump control for desalination. Solar-variable adaptation of Siemens VFD systems.
